content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(ii) WARN — NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription is a literal "TODO" placeholder (App/Info.plist), and NSCameraUsageDescription ("This app needs camera access.") is generic and doesn't mention the actual feature (document scanning, per ScannerView.swift's "Point the camera at a document").
Pierre: Apple reviewers reject apps with placeholder or generic purpose strings because users can't understand why the permission is needed, and "TODO" is an obvious sign the app wasn't finished. Also, there's no visible location-based feature in the provided code, so it's unclear why location is requested at all. Fix both strings to be specific, e.g. "SnapScan uses your camera to scan documents" and either remove the location key or explain its actual use tied to a visible feature.
